---
title: "Academy — Contributor Track"
subtitle: "Land a canon-compliant PR without review blockers"
date: today
date-format: "MMMM D, YYYY"
description: "Stage-by-stage walkthrough of the UIAO_125 Contributor track. For developers, canon stewards, and CI engineers working inside the substrate."
---

The Contributor track comes out of [UIAO_125 §1.2](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/specs/program-training.md) unchanged — this page is the reader-facing walkthrough, not a parallel curriculum. Each stage names what to **read**, what to **do**, and how to **verify**. Gate evidence for stage N is a prerequisite for stage N+1.

## Prerequisites (shared core)

Before stage 1, finish the shared core in [UIAO_125 §1.1](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/specs/program-training.md): substrate orientation, vocabulary, artifact literacy, governance flow. Those four modules take ~90 minutes.

---

## Stage 1 — Foundation read

**Read:**

- [UIAO_001 SSOT](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/UIAO-SSOT.md)
- [UIAO_003 Adapter Segmentation Overview](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/UIAO_003_Adapter_Segmentation_Overview_v1.0.md) — the dual-axis `class` × `mission-class` taxonomy you'll live inside
- [UIAO_200 Substrate Manifest](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/substrate-manifest.yaml) — the three-module topology
- [UIAO_201 Workspace Contract](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/workspace-contract.yaml) — how `$UIAO_WORKSPACE_ROOT` resolves

**Do:** clone the repo, run `uiao substrate walk`. Read the output.

**Verify:** exit code 0. Zero DRIFT-SCHEMA or DRIFT-PROVENANCE findings. You now know what a clean substrate looks like.

---

## Stage 2 — Canon change workflow

**Read:**

- [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) — the canon-change protocol
- [ADR-028](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/adr/adr-028-monorepo-consolidation-gos-integration.md) — a real ADR to study
- [ADR-030](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/adr/adr-030-pre-uiao-terminology-reconciliation.md) — the pre-UIAO promotion path you'll follow for any inbox-origin content

**Do:** write and land a small ADR — a doctrinal change, however minor, that you can defend. Use the existing ADRs as templates.

**Verify:** the ADR lands on `main`. CI is green. At least one named other-contributor has commented on the decision in the PR.

---

## Stage 3 — Schema authoring and validation

**Read:**

- [`src/uiao/schemas/`](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/tree/main/src/uiao/schemas) — the five JSON Schemas that validate every registry, manifest, and frontmatter edit
- [`scripts/validate_schemas.py`](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/scripts/validate_schemas.py) — how CI runs validation locally

**Do:** write a new JSON Schema (or meaningfully extend an existing one). Validate it against a fixture in the corresponding registry.

**Verify:** `make schemas` exits 0. The registry validates cleanly against your schema changes.

---

## Stage 4 — Adapter development

**Read:**

- [UIAO_121 Adapter Conformance Test Plan — Template](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/specs/adapter-conformance-test-plan-template.md)
- [UIAO_123 Adapter Integration Test Plan — Canonical Template](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/specs/adapter-integration-test-plan.md)
- [UIAO_124 Adapter Operations Runbook](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/specs/adapter-operations-runbook.md)
- [UIAO_131 Adapter Test Strategy](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/specs/adapter-test-strategy.md) — the three-tier model

**Do:** build a new adapter against the `modernization-registry.yaml` schema. Produce:
- Tier 1 (live commercial tenant) evidence — run against a [Microsoft 365 Developer Program tenant](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/office/developer-program/microsoft-365-developer-program-faq) or a vendor dev program
- Tier 2 (contract fixtures) evidence — under `tests/fixtures/contract/<your-adapter>/`

**Verify:** UIAO_121 conformance template filled for the adapter. Tier 1 + Tier 2 both green. Registry entry advances from `draft` → `beta`.

---

## Stage 5 — Release engineering

**Read:** [UIAO_118 Release Engineering](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/specs/release.md).

**Do:** cut a release — tag triggers `release.yml`, which produces a signed wheel, a CycloneDX SBOM, and a sigstore bundle.

**Verify:** tag pushed to origin, release workflow green, release assets attached with three signature files. Matches the `v0.2.1` pattern from the current release.

---

## Completion

You're done with the Contributor track when you've landed a PR that touches **at least one canon artifact, at least one schema, and at least one test** — and CI is green. That's the criterion in [UIAO_125 §3](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/specs/program-training.md).

## Adjacent paths

- [Operator track](operator-track.qmd) — complementary, not mutually exclusive
- [Comic tour](comics.qmd) — a 45-minute flyover if you want the mental model before diving deeper
- [Substrate Status](../docs/substrate-status.qmd) — the honest reality map at any point
